text.title: Cloud Waste Elimination and Non-Production Scheduling Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: All non-production environments (dev, staging, QA, sandbox) MUST implement scheduled shutdown during off-hours. Minimum: stop 20:00, start 06:00 weekdays; stop Fri 20:00, start Mon 06:00 weekends. Target 70% cost reduction on non-prod compute.
rule.rationale: Running non-prod 24/7 when used 9h/day wastes 65-70% of compute cost; scheduled shutdown is the fastest FinOps ROI available.

rule.statement: Implement automated idle resource detection running weekly. Alerts MUST include resource ID, age, estimated monthly cost, and last-active date. Cleanup MUST require human approval for production; non-prod cleanup may auto-execute with a 7-day warning period.
rule.rationale: Manual audits decay within 3-6 months — automation keeps the pipeline clean continuously.

rule.statement: Every cleanup script MUST default to dry-run mode and require an explicit `--apply` flag to mutate. Output a per-resource preview (id, age, cost, action) before any destructive call.
rule.rationale: Running cleanup without preview destroys legitimate resources and erodes trust in FinOps automation; trust loss is permanent.

rule.statement: Resources exempt from waste cleanup MUST carry a tag `waste-exception=<reason>` with an owner email and an expiry date. Untagged exemptions are rejected by the scanner.
rule.rationale: Untagged exceptions become institutional dark matter — nobody knows why a resource is 'special' six months later.

rule.statement: Target overall cloud waste rate ≤25% of baseline spend; untagged resources ≤5%; non-prod scheduled savings ≥70% of non-prod compute. Track monthly and break the build (CI cost gate) on regression beyond a 5% MoM drift.
rule.rationale: Without numeric targets the program slides into theatre; KPI gates force evidence-based progress.

rule.statement: Skip when cloud spend <$5k/month OR all non-prod already on schedule with waste-rate <30% OR no authority to schedule shutdowns / approve deletes.
rule.rationale: Below threshold the overhead outweighs the savings; above-threshold prerequisites must hold or the method cannot execute.
